findings.description: reachable() never guards on 'if neighbor not in seen' before enqueuing; on any cycle (which the docstring explicitly permits — 'possibly cyclic graph') nodes are re-enqueued indefinitely, producing an infinite loop that never terminates.

The BFS start node is never added to 'seen', so if 'start' is part of a cycle it gets re-enqueued when a neighbor points back to it, compounding the infinite-loop defect even if a guard were added for other nodes.

count_paths() is a plain unbounded recursion with no cycle detection; on any graph with a cycle it recurses infinitely and raises RecursionError, despite the caller having no way to enforce the 'DAG assumed' precondition at the call site.

count_paths() has no memoization; on a valid DAG with diamond-shaped sub-paths it recomputes identical sub-problems exponentially (2^n calls for n-layer diamond), making it unusable for any non-trivial dependency graph — defer detail to critic-performance.

neighbors() reads self._edges[node] on a defaultdict; for any node that has never been a source this silently auto-creates an empty adjacency-list entry, mutating internal graph state as a side-effect of a read-only query and polluting subsequent topological_order() or reachable() iterations.

reachable() silently returns an empty set when 'start' is not present in self._nodes or self._edges, giving the caller no signal to distinguish 'node exists but has no outgoing edges' from 'node does not exist at all'.
